title: More than 200 rescued from IS-linked group in DR Congo
content: The youngest of those freed from an Allied Democratic Forces camp is a girl aged just 14, Uganda's army says.

originalTitle: Black beauty queen who represented South Africa at Miss World during apartheid dies aged 76
title: Cynthia Shange: Our Brave Star Who Shone Bright!
content:
A Sad Day for South Africa

Hello, my little friends! Today, we have a story about a very special lady from our home, South Africa. Her name was Cynthia Shange. She was a beautiful and brave woman. We are sad to say that Cynthia Shange has passed away. She was 76 years old. She lived a long and wonderful life, and she did something very important for our country.

Who Was Cynthia Shange?

Cynthia Shange was born a long, long time ago. When she was a young woman, she was very beautiful and kind. People noticed her lovely smile and her gentle heart. She loved her country, South Africa, very much. When she was young, she took part in beauty contests. These are shows where people celebrate beauty and talent. Cynthia was a real star!

What is Miss World?

Did you know that there is a very big beauty show called Miss World? It’s a huge competition where beautiful women from all over the world come together. They wear pretty dresses, show their talents, and talk about important things in their countries. It’s like a big party where countries share their beauty! Many people watch it on TV. It's a very famous show.

A Special Trip to Miss World

In 1972, a very special thing happened. Cynthia Shange went to the Miss World contest! Imagine, a lady from South Africa going to a big show like that. It was held in a far-away country called England. She packed her bags and traveled across the ocean. She was ready to represent our amazing country!

Did You Know?

Did you know that the Miss World contest started way back in 1951? That’s even before your parents or grandparents were born! It’s one of the oldest and most popular beauty contests in the whole world. Countries like India, Nigeria, and even South Africa have had winners!

A Time of Apartheid

Now, here's a very important part of Cynthia's story. When Cynthia went to Miss World, South Africa was going through a difficult time. This time was called "Apartheid." It means "apart-ness" in Afrikaans, which is a language spoken in South Africa. During Apartheid, there were some very unfair rules.

Unfair Rules

Imagine if the rules said you couldn't play with your friend just because they had different coloured hair, or different coloured skin. That’s a bit like what Apartheid was. People were treated differently because of the colour of their skin. Black people, white people, and coloured people were kept apart. They couldn't always go to the same schools, live in the same places, or even sit on the same benches. It was not fair at all.

Did You Know?

Did you know that the word "apartheid" means "separateness" in Afrikaans? It was a very sad time in South Africa's history, but people like Cynthia Shange helped make things better. It reminds us why it's so important to treat everyone with kindness and respect, no matter what they look like.

Cynthia’s Brave Step

During Apartheid, it was very unusual for a black woman like Cynthia to represent South Africa on a big world stage. But Cynthia was brave! She showed the world that beauty comes in all colours. She showed that black South Africans were also talented and wonderful. She stood tall and proud for all of us.

Two South African Stars

At the Miss World show in 1972, there was something else very special. South Africa sent *two* contestants! One was Cynthia Shange, who was black. And the other was a white lady. This was very unusual for that time. It was like showing the world that even though South Africa had unfair rules, there was beauty and talent in all its people.

A Message to the World

By sending both a black and a white contestant, South Africa was sending a message. It was a small step, but an important one. It showed that maybe, just maybe, things could change. Cynthia Shange was a part of that important message. She was a shining example of strength and grace.

Did You Know?

Did you know that South Africa has 11 official languages? That means people speak many different languages in our country! This shows how diverse and wonderful our nation is, with people from many backgrounds living together.

What Happened After Miss World?

After coming back from Miss World, Cynthia Shange continued to be a star! She didn't just stop at being a beauty queen. She became an actress! An actress is someone who acts in movies or TV shows. She starred in popular TV shows and films in South Africa. Many people knew her face from their television screens. She brought joy to many homes with her acting.

A Shining Example

Cynthia Shange was more than just a beautiful face. She was a symbol of hope and change. She showed everyone that you can be proud of who you are and where you come from. Even in hard times, she stood up and shone brightly. She helped pave the way for a better South Africa, where everyone is treated fairly.

Did You Know?

Did you know that South Africa is sometimes called the "Rainbow Nation"? This is because after Apartheid ended, people from all different colours and cultures came together to build a new, fair country. Like a rainbow has many colours, our nation has many wonderful people!

Remembering Cynthia

Today, we remember Cynthia Shange as a hero. She was a beautiful lady who used her platform to show the world the real South Africa. She made us proud. We thank her for her bravery and for being a shining light during a challenging time. Let's all remember to be kind to each other and celebrate our differences, just like Cynthia taught us by her example. Her story teaches us that even small steps can make a big difference for a better future. She will always be a star in our hearts.
summary: Cynthia Shange, a brave lady from South Africa, who showed the world how beautiful and strong our country is during a time of unfair rules, has passed away.
whyMatters: Her story is important because it teaches us to be brave, celebrate everyone's beauty, and always treat each other with kindness, even when things are difficult.

originalTitle: Nigerian wins global prize for trying to save bats in a country that shuns them
title: A Super Hero for Bats in Nigeria Wins a Big Award!
content:
Hello, Super Kids of Africa!

Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Do you know what makes a real hero? It's someone who is brave and kind, and helps others, even when it's tough. Today, we have a super special story about a wonderful woman from Nigeria who is a true hero for our animal friends! Her name is Iroro Tanshi, and she loves nature very much. She is like a detective for plants and animals, and she has a big heart for creatures that sometimes people don't understand.

Meet Our Nature Hero, Iroro!

Iroro Tanshi is from Nigeria, one of our big, beautiful countries. She is a scientist who studies nature. We can call her a 'nature helper' because that's what she does every day. She spends her time learning about animals and plants, and how they live together. Her favourite animals to study are... bats! Yes, those amazing creatures that fly in the night!

Why Do People Worry About Bats?

Now, let's talk about bats! What do you think of when you hear 'bats'? Some people might think bats are a bit scary. Maybe because they fly when it's dark! In some parts of Nigeria, and other places, grown-ups have old stories about bats. Sometimes, these stories make people think bats are linked to bad magic or bring bad luck. This makes it hard for bats to live safely. People might not want them around their homes or farms. They might even try to chase them away or hurt them.

But Bats Are Super Helpers!

But Iroro knew the truth. She knew that bats are not scary at all! They are very important helpers in nature, just like bees that help flowers or birds that eat bugs. Did you know that bats are the *only* flying mammals? Yes! They have fur like a cat or a dog, not feathers like a bird! Their wings are made of skin, stretched between their long fingers. It's like having super stretchy hands for flying! There are many different kinds of bats. Some bats love to eat yummy fruits. When they eat fruit, they drop the seeds. These seeds can grow into new trees! This helps our forests stay healthy and green, giving us fresh air. Other bats are like tiny superheroes that eat bugs. They fly around at night and catch mosquitoes and other insects. Did you know a single bat can eat hundreds of mosquitoes in just one night? Wow! This helps keep us healthy by eating bugs that can bite us and spread sickness.

Iroro's Brave Mission to Save Bats

Because Iroro knew how special bats are, she decided to be their champion! Even though some people were scared of bats, she was not afraid. She knew she had to help. Her big mission was to teach people that bats are friends, not foes. She goes to different places and talks to grown-ups and kids, just like you! She tells them how bats help our farms, our trees, and even us! Iroro also works very hard to find special kinds of bats. Some bats are very rare, which means there are not many of them left in the world. She wants to make sure these special bats have safe homes. She goes into dark caves and explores forests to find where bats live. She found a kind of bat that people thought was gone forever! It's called the *short-nosed fruit bat*. This was a super amazing discovery! It means we can now help these special bats more, so they don't disappear from our world. Iroro helps protect the places where bats live, like important caves.

A Big Prize for a Big Heart!

Because Iroro worked so hard and was so brave, she won a very big award! It's called the Future for Nature Award. This is a special prize that people from all over the world give to someone who helps nature. People from many countries said, "Wow, Iroro is doing amazing work! She is a true hero for bats!" She won a lot of money to help her save even more bats and teach more people. This award shows that her kindness and bravery are seen and loved by people everywhere. It makes Africa very proud!

What Can We Learn from Iroro?

Iroro teaches us many important lessons. She shows us that all animals are important, even the ones some people don't understand or might be a little scared of. She teaches us to learn about nature and to be kind to every living thing. And most importantly, she shows us that one person, with a big heart and a brave spirit, can make a huge difference in the world! We can all be like Iroro. We can learn about the animals around us, talk to our friends and family about why they are important, and help protect our beautiful planet.

More Fun Bat Facts!

Did you know that some bats can fly super fast, up to 100 miles per hour? That's faster than a car on the highway! Bats live in big groups sometimes, like a big family! These groups are called colonies. Some colonies can have millions of bats! Bats use special sounds to find their way in the dark. It's like having super sonar! This is called echolocation. They make tiny squeaks and listen for the echoes to tell them where things are. So let's cheer for Iroro Tanshi, our bat-saving hero from Nigeria! She helps us all remember to protect our animal friends and the wonderful nature we share.
summary: A brave Nigerian woman named Iroro Tanshi won a big award from all over the world for helping bats, even though many people thought bats were scary, teaching everyone that bats are very important helpers in nature.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us to be kind to all animals, even if they look different or people have scary stories about them, and it shows us that one person can make a big, positive change to protect nature and our animal friends.

originalTitle: Iran war: What is happening on day 53 of the US-Israel conflict?
title: Let's Learn About Countries Talking for Peace!
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends from Africa!

Jambo! That means 'hello' in Swahili, a language many people speak here in Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I'm here to tell you about something important happening far, far away. Sometimes, big countries have a problem, just like you and your friend might have a small problem sometimes. And just like you, grown-ups try to solve these problems by talking! Let's learn about it together.

What Are Countries?

Imagine your home. It's a special place, right? Well, a country is like a really, really big home for many, many people! Each country has its own name, like Kenya, Nigeria, or South Africa here in our wonderful continent. Far away, there are other countries like the United States of America (we can call it USA), Israel, and Iran. Each country has a leader, like the head of a big family, who makes important choices for everyone living there. Their job is to keep everyone safe and happy. Did you know? There are almost 200 countries in the world! Each one has its own flag, like a special picture that tells us who they are.

Big Countries Having a Problem

Sometimes, countries don't agree on things. It's like when you and your friend want to play a different game. This can happen between big countries too. Right now, some countries, like the USA, Israel, and Iran, are having a big disagreement. We don't need to know all the tiny details of the problem right now, because it's very grown-up stuff. What's important to know is that they are not agreeing, and people want them to be friends again, or at least to understand each other.

Talking to Solve Problems

What do you do when you have a problem with a friend? You talk, right? You try to listen to each other and find a way to be happy together. That's exactly what countries try to do! When leaders from different countries sit down and talk to try and fix a problem, we call it 'diplomacy'. It's like a big, important conversation to make sure everyone is safe and happy. Many clever and kind people work very hard to make sure countries talk to each other instead of letting problems get bigger. Did you know? Talking is often the best way to solve almost any problem, big or small! It helps everyone understand each other better.

Why the Talking Has Stopped For Now

So, people who want peace have been trying to get these countries – the USA, Israel, and Iran – to sit down and talk. They want to help them find a way to agree and stop having this big problem. But for now, the talking has *stopped*. The grown-ups say the talks are 'stalled'. Imagine a car that's stuck in the mud and can't move forward – that's what 'stalled' means! It's not going anywhere right now. One of the countries, Iran, said that they don't want to talk right now because they feel like someone is pushing them too hard. When you feel 'pressure', it's like someone is trying to make you do something you don't want to do. So, they said 'no' to talking for now. Did you know? Even when talking stops, people don't give up hope! Many good people around the world keep trying to find new ways for countries to talk again.

Why Is Peace So Important?

Peace means everyone can live safely, go to school, play with friends, and sleep soundly at night. When countries have problems that are not solved by talking, it can make people in those countries feel worried. We all want peace because peace means happy homes, happy schools, and happy communities. It means we can all learn and grow without fear. Did you know? The United Nations is like a super big club for almost all the countries in the world! They meet to talk about peace and help each other.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are small, we can help too! We can be kind to our friends. We can listen to each other when we have disagreements. We can try to solve our own little problems by talking, just like we hope the big countries will do. Every kind word and every friendly talk helps make our world a happier place. So, remember, even when things are 'stalled', we always hope for a brighter tomorrow where everyone talks and finds solutions. Let's send big hopes for peace to all the countries in the world! That's all for today's news. Keep learning, keep smiling, and keep being kind! Kwaheri! (That means 'goodbye' in Swahili!)
summary: Big countries like the USA, Israel, and Iran are having a disagreement, and even though people tried to help them talk for peace, the talks have stopped for now because one country felt pushed.
whyMatters: It matters because when big countries don't agree and stop talking, it can make people feel worried. Talking for peace helps everyone stay safe and happy in their homes and communities.

originalTitle: Technofacism? Why Palantir’s pro-West ‘manifesto’ has critics alarmed
title: Super Smart Computers: Fun or a Little Scary?
content:
Hello, my amazing friends all the way from across Africa! It’s your kid-friendly reporter here, ready to share some really cool, but also a little bit important, news with you today. We're going to talk about super-smart computers and a big company that makes them. Are you ready? Let’s dive in!

What Are We Talking About Today?

Today, we’re going to talk about a big computer company. Its name is Palantir. This company makes very, very smart computer programs. These programs are so smart, they are like super-brains inside computers! This company wrote a special message. It was like a big plan about how they want to use their super-smart computers. But guess what? Some people are a little bit worried about their plan. We’re going to find out why!

Meet Palantir: The Big Computer Company

Imagine you have a giant puzzle. It has millions of pieces! It’s super hard to solve. Palantir makes computer programs that can help put those millions of pieces together super fast. They help big groups like governments or other big companies. These programs can look at lots and lots of information. Then they help people understand what all that information means. It’s like having a super helper that can sort through everything! Did you know? Computers can do amazing things! They can help us learn, play games, and even talk to our friends and family far away. They are a big part of our world now.

What is a 'Manifesto'?

Now, Palantir wrote something called a 'manifesto.' That's a big, fancy word! But it just means a special message or a big plan. It’s like when you make a plan for your soccer team, saying how you want to play and what you want to achieve. Palantir wrote down their big plan for how they want to use their smart computer programs. They wanted everyone to know what they believe is important when using these powerful computers.

Palantir's Special Message

In their message, Palantir said they want to help their friends. They said they want to work with countries that are like them. These countries are mostly in places like America and Europe. They want to use their super-smart computers to help these friends stay safe and strong. It's like choosing which team you want to play with and help win the game. This sounds good, right? Helping friends is always nice. But some people heard this and thought, 'Hmm, maybe we should think about this a little more carefully.' Did you know? Africa has many smart people who are also making great computer programs and apps! They help solve problems in our communities, like learning or finding food.

Why Are Some Grown-Ups Worried?

This is where it gets a little bit important to understand. Some smart grown-ups read Palantir's message and became worried. They worried because super-smart computers, often called AI (which stands for Artificial Intelligence), are becoming very powerful. They can do so many things! If these powerful computers only help one side or one group of friends, what about everyone else? What if it's not fair to everyone?

The Idea of 'Too Much Power'

Imagine a very strong robot. This robot can do anything you ask. It’s super helpful! But what if only one person can tell the robot what to do? And what if that one person tells the robot to do things that are only good for them, and not for you or your friends? That wouldn't be very fair, would it? Some people are worried that these super-smart computers, if not used carefully, could give too much power to just a few people or countries. This could make things unfair for many others. Did you know? Fairness is very important in games and in life! It means everyone gets a chance and things are shared properly.

Smart Computers Making Big Choices?

The biggest worry is about super-smart computers making very important choices all by themselves. We want humans to make the really big decisions, don't we? If computers get too smart and too powerful, they might start making choices for us. What if they decide what's best without asking people? This could be like a computer deciding what you should eat for dinner every day, or what games you are allowed to play. We want to make our own choices, right?

What Does 'Technofascism' Mean? (A Big, Scary Word Simplified)

One of the really big, scary words some grown-ups used was 'technofascism.' Wow, that's a mouthful! But don't worry, we won't use that word again. What they meant was: if super-powerful technology (like these smart computers) is used to control people, to make unfair rules, or to watch people too much, it could take away people's freedom. It's like someone using a super-duper spying device to know everything about everyone, all the time. That would feel very uncomfortable and not free at all!

Why Does This Matter to Us in Africa?

Even if Palantir is a company far away, these smart computers are everywhere now. They are in our phones, in our schools, and helping us in many ways. It's important for all of us, especially you smart kids, to think about how we use computers and how these powerful tools should be used. We want them to help *everyone*, not just a few. We want them to make our world a better and fairer place. We want them to help us learn and grow, not control us or make things unfair. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, people are using simple technology, like mobile phones, to do amazing things – like sending money to family or getting important health information!

What Can We Do?

So, what can we do? We can be smart computer users! Here are some ideas: * **Ask questions:** Always ask grown-ups if something on a computer is fair or safe. * **Share fairly:** Remember to share computer time and games with your friends. * **Be kind online:** Always be kind and respectful when you use computers or tablets to talk to people. * **Think big:** Think about how computers can help *everyone* in your community and your country.

My Final Thoughts

Computers and smart programs are truly amazing! They can help us do so many wonderful things. But like any powerful tool, we need to use them with care and wisdom. Let’s all promise to use our smart brains and smart computers to make the world a better, fairer, and happier place for everyone. Until next time, stay curious and keep learning!
summary: A big computer company called Palantir made a special plan about using super-smart computers, but some people are worried that these powerful tools might not be used fairly for everyone if not controlled wisely.
whyMatters: It matters because super-smart computers are becoming very strong, and we need to make sure they are used to help everyone and not just a few, so that our world stays fair and free for all of us.

originalTitle: Israeli police arrest two ultra-Orthodox Jews for removing Israeli flags
title: Two Men Arrested for Taking Down Flags Far Away!
content:
Hello from Your African News Reporter!

Hi everyone in Africa! It’s your friend, your kid-friendly news reporter, here to share a story from a country far, far away. We learn about many different things and places in our news show. Today, we have a story about flags and rules. It's important to understand that people live differently all over the world, but rules help everyone get along.

What Happened Far Away in a Town Called Beit Shemesh?

Imagine a town called Beit Shemesh. It’s a real town, a bit like your town or city, but it's very far away in a country named Israel. In this town, there were some flags. You know, like the beautiful flag of your own country! But two men, who live in Beit Shemesh, did something that wasn't allowed. They saw the flags of Israel and they pulled them down. They took them off the poles where they were flying high. It's a bit like someone pulling down a picture from a wall when it's not theirs to pull down. They didn't want the flags to be there, so they took them away.

What is a Flag and Why Do Countries Have Them?

A flag is a very special piece of cloth. It usually has special colours and shapes on it. Every country in the world has its own flag! Your country has one, my country has one, and the country of Israel has one too. A flag is like a big sign that tells everyone, 'This is our country!' or 'This is who we are!'. Flags make people feel proud of their country. They fly high on poles, on buildings, and sometimes you see them during special celebrations. Taking down a flag, especially a country's flag, can make many people feel sad or angry because it is a very important symbol. Did you know? The colours and symbols on flags often tell a story about the country's history or what its people believe in! For example, some colours might mean peace, and others might mean bravery.

Who Are These Special People?

The two men who took down the flags are part of a special group of people. They are called "ultra-Orthodox Jews." These are people who follow their very old traditions and religious rules very, very strictly. They live their lives following these ancient customs, which are very important to them. They often dress in special clothes. The men might wear dark suits and hats, and the women might wear long dresses and head coverings. Their life is guided by their faith and how they understand their religion, which has been passed down for thousands of years. They are a big part of the community in places like Beit Shemesh. Did you know? Many religions have special ways of dressing or living that are very important to their followers. It’s part of their identity, just like your culture is part of yours!

Why Didn't They Like the Flags?

This is a bit tricky, but I will try to explain it simply. These ultra-Orthodox Jews have very, very old ideas about their country and their religion. For their special reasons, they do not like the flag of Israel. They believe that their country should only have a king or a special leader sent by God, and not a modern government and its flag. So, when they saw the flags of Israel, they felt they shouldn't be there because of their deep beliefs. It's like when sometimes friends have different ideas about what is right or wrong. For them, taking down the flags was a way to show their beliefs. They really, really feel strongly about their traditions.

What Happened After They Took the Flags Down?

Because taking down flags is against the rules in Israel, something else happened next. The police came! The police are the people whose job it is to keep everyone safe and make sure people follow the rules of the country. When someone does something that breaks a rule or a law, the police will come to see what happened. The Israeli police came to Beit Shemesh and they found the two men. They then arrested them. To be arrested means the police took them to the police station. This happens when someone is suspected of breaking a rule. They will then talk to the police about what they did.

Why Rules Are Important for Everyone

Every country, every town, and even every home has rules. Rules help us to live together in peace. Imagine if there were no rules at all! Everyone might do whatever they want, and that could cause problems and make people sad or hurt. Rules help us know what we can and cannot do. They keep us safe and fair. In this story, taking down a country's flag is against the rules in Israel. Even if someone has very strong beliefs and doesn't like the flag, they still need to follow the country's laws. It is important for everyone to respect the rules, even when they disagree with something. We can talk about our disagreements, but we must still obey the laws. Did you know? Even in your school, you have rules! Like sharing toys or not running in the hallways. These rules help everyone have a good and safe time at school!

Learning from This Story

So, what can we learn from this story from far away? We learn that people have different ideas and beliefs. We also learn that rules are very important for living together in a community. Even when people disagree, they must still follow the laws of their country. That helps keep peace and order. It’s always important to be kind and understand that people are different. We can learn about each other's ways. But we must also remember that rules are there for a reason, to keep everyone safe and to respect each other's symbols, like flags.

Goodbye for Now!

That’s all for today’s news from your kid-friendly reporter in Africa! Keep exploring, keep learning, and keep being kind to everyone around you. See you next time!
summary: Two ultra-Orthodox Jewish men in Israel were arrested by police for taking down Israeli flags because they have different beliefs about their country.
whyMatters: This story shows us that even when people have very strong different ideas, it's important to follow the rules of the country to live together peacefully.

originalTitle: Ranking the most successful world record transfers of the past 50 years
title: Ranking the most successful world record transfers of the past 50 years
content: Stumping up world record sums doesn't always guarantee success, but plenty of football's most expensive players have delivered on their hefty fees -  here are the top 10.
